what_it_does: Records a verified structural finding about the noticeboard store: each item is a markdown file whose filename equals its id and the address minus the board:// scheme, which makes board:// edges expressible as Obsidian wikilinks without a lookup table, and it inventories the resulting edge counts and item types.
observations: Frontmatter declares id, address, type block, source claude_code, channel dragnet-development, and a part_of link to board://item-4baaab55.
The body is titled '0 · The one fact that makes this whole thing tractable' and is marked [Verified].
Claims a 1:1 identity: item-XXXX.md ↔ id: item-XXXX ↔ address: board://item-XXXX.
Cites _render and cc_board.py:175-178 as the rendering/code context for markdown + YAML frontmatter + body.
Counts 206 internal target: board:// edges that map directly to Obsidian [[item-XXX]] wikilinks.
Counts roughly 50 cross-scheme edges with no vault file: 21 decision://, 12 image://, 8 code://, 8 session://, 1 ui://.
Notes 10 items already contain [[…]] body-level wikilinks.
Lists type distribution across 265 live items: 123 block, 36 guide, 26 request, 24 signal, 19 note, 12 idea, 11 document, 8 tip, 6 issue, 1 annotation.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, suggesting an initial filing with no later edits.
standouts: The filename-id-address identity is presented as the single enabler that keeps an Obsidian shim thin rather than a full translation engine.
The dangling-node problem is explicitly quantified by scheme rather than left vague.
The corpus already contains body-level wikilinks, so Obsidian link syntax is not foreign to the store.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable id, address, type, source, channel, and typed links.
Markdown body uses section heading, [Verified] tag, and bold-lead bullet lists.
References source code by file and line range for traceability.
Addresses use board:// scheme; filenames strip the scheme to become note names.
Type taxonomy is stored as a scalar frontmatter field suitable for Dataview/Bases queries.
concerns: The metrics are a one-time grep snapshot over 265 live items and may drift as the corpus changes.
Line-number references to cc_board.py can become stale if the renderer changes.
The cross-scheme counts are asserted to have 'no vault file' without showing the exclusion criteria in the file.
notes: A concise verified finding that the noticeboard's filename-id-address identity makes Obsidian wikilink shimming thin and sizes the remaining dangling cross-scheme edge cleanup.
questions: What is the specific 'shim' this tractability is meant to enable?
What does the part_of target board://item-4baaab55 describe?
How exactly were the 265 'live items' and the edge counts produced?
What is _render and what role does cc_board.py:175-178 play?
How was it determined that each cross-scheme target has no corresponding vault file?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the title frames one fact as making the work tractable, and the file delivers that fact plus supporting counts and edge classifications.
intention_signals: It appears to be making the case that integrating or exporting the noticeboard to Obsidian should require only a thin shim because the store already uses markdown files whose names match ids, and to scope the real remaining work to cleaning up about fifty cross-scheme dangling edges.
novelty: It reframes a custom knowledge store as already Obsidian-compatible due to a filename-id-address identity, and quantifies the non-trivial residue as dangling cross-scheme nodes rather than a general address-translation problem.
contribution: A documented invariant and a quantified inventory of internal edges, dangling cross-scheme edges, existing body wikilinks, and item-type distribution that a fused system can use as a baseline for Obsidian shim or migration design.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item documenting the verified 1:1 identity between noticeboard markdown filenames, item ids, and board:// addresses, and quantifying 206 internal edges, about 50 dangling cross-scheme edges, 10 body wikilinks, and item type counts across 265 live items.
intention_for_embedding: Establish that the store is already Obsidian-native enough to make wikilink shimming thin while sizing the remaining dangling-node cleanup.
